history.content:
<thought>
I am facing upward. The cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward would be fatal. The safest immediate move is to turn right, which should orient me toward the open floor cell to my right so I can navigate around the lava toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing upward. The cell directly ahead is a safe floor cell, while the cells to my left and right are walls. Since I cannot turn into a useful open path immediately, the best next step is to move forward into the open space above me, which keeps me progressing toward the visible goal and away from lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible at B6, which is one cell to my left. Since I am facing upward (the ^ marker), turning left will make me face the goal, and the path in that direction is safe for the next move.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D, facing up. The cell directly ahead is row 06 column D, which is safe floor. Moving forward keeps me inside the open area and brings me closer to the goal at row 05 column E, while avoiding the lava at row 06 column C and row 06 column E.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing upward, and the cell directly ahead contains lava, so moving forward would be fatal. The safest immediate move is to turn to face a safe floor cell instead of lava. Turning right will orient me toward the open path on the east side.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is a safe floor tile, while the goal is still two cells ahead and one cell to the left from my current position. Moving forward keeps me progressing toward the goal and avoids the lava at the goal’s right side.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
